channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: China's leader makes rare visit to province facing Taiwan following major war exercises
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — China’s President Xi Jinping made a rare visit to a province facing Taiwan following military exercises that mobilized China's navy, air force, missile force and land troops to simulate a blockade of the self-governing island democracy that Beijing claims as its own territory a
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Michelle Obama will headline an Atlanta rally aimed at boosting voter turnout
item.description: Former first lady Michelle Obama is headlining a rally in Atlanta alongside celebrities and local civic leaders focusing on engaging younger and first-time voters, as well as voters of color.
item.dc:creator: Matt Brown, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Menendez brothers' family to push for their release as prosecutors review 1989 case
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The extended family of Erik and Lyle Menendez will advocate for the brothers' release from prison during a news conference set for Wednesday in downtown Los Angeles as prosecutors review new evidence to determine whether they should be serving life sentences for killing their pare
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Republicans challenge more than 63,000 voters in Georgia, but few removed, AP finds
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — From Georgia's mountains to its Atlantic shore, challenges to the qualifications of voters have rolled in this summer and fall, part of a wide-ranging national effort coordinated by Donald Trump’s allies to enlist Republican activists to remove people they view as suspect from the vot
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY and CHARLOTTE KRAMON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Small business disaster loan program is out of money until Congress approves new funds
item.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson assured there would be support to provide necessary funds – when Congress returns after the November election.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump: Black and Hispanic voters who vote for Harris 'gotta have your head examined'
item.description: The former president suggested any Black or Hispanic voters who do not support him should have their head examined, echoing a similar comment he has made about Jewish voters backing Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Diddy accuser: He 'had something to do' with Tupac Shakur murder
item.description: Diddy accuser Ashley Parham: Rape was "payback" for saying she believed he "had something to do with the murder" of Tupac Shakur.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Unions face a moment of truth in Michigan in this year's presidential race
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris and former President Donald Trump are appealing to middle-class workers in very different ways.
item.dc:creator: Joey Cappelletti And Matt Brown, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The son of a South Carolina inmate urges the governor to save his father from execution
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — Richard Moore never meant to kill anyone the night he robbed a South Carolina convenience store and the Black man was convicted by a jury with no African Americans, his son and lawyers say as they fight to save the inmate from execution next month.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: In the heartland of Mexico's Sinaloa cartel, the old ways have changed and violence rages
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Cellphone chats have become death sentences in the continuing, bloody factional war inside Mexico's Sinaloa drug cartel. Cartel gunmen stop youths on the street or in their cars and demand their phones.
item.dc:creator: MARK STEVENSON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Gun-wielding suspect gets beaten up by employees at pizzeria he tried to rob: police
item.description: A 19-year-old suspect in Texas was arrested after he allegedly tried to rob a Marco's Pizza location in Austin. Employees at the restaurant beat him up after he showed a gun.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Prosecutors will not file criminal charges against 2 people at center of Los Angeles racism scandal
item.description: The Los Angeles city attorney’s office says it does not plan to file criminal charges against two people who were investigated in connection with the unlawful recording of a racist conversation that rocked City Hall in 2022 and prompted the city council president to resign after the audio was leaked
item.dc:creator: Stefanie Dazio, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: DNA forensics helps identify remains found in Colorado freezer as teenager missing for nearly 20 years
item.description: The Mesa County Sheriff's Office announced that remains found inside a freezer in January have been identified as those from a 16-year-old girl who went missing in 2005.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Coca-Cola recalls 'zero sugar' lemonade after it's found to contain full sugar
item.description: The recall, which Coca-Cola issued in September but didn’t announce publicly, applied to 13,152 cases, each with 12 cartons of 12 cans of 12 ounces of lemonade.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, ABC 4

item.title: Can Apple Watch 10 be used as a medical device?
item.description: It's increasingly clear that the future of the Apple Watch isn’t just as an alternative to cell phones.
item.dc:creator: David Lazarus, NewsNation

item.title: Rufus Wainwright blasts Trump playing 'Hallelujah' at town hall: 'The height of blasphemy'
item.description: The 51-year-old singer said Tuesday that he was "horrified" to learn his 2001 cover of Leonard Cohen's "Hallelujah" was part of the playlist at the Trump campaign event a night earlier.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, Fox 5

item.title: Trump clashes with Bloomberg editor over tariffs
item.description: “That is going to have a serious effect on the overall economy,” Bloomberg’s top editor, John Micklethwait said.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: What donations Hurricane Helene victims in western NC need as seasons change
item.description: Alicia Stemper, a public information officer for Avery County Emergency Operations Center, said high-priority donations include laundry detergent, heaters and mold removal products.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Geraldo Rivera backing Harris: Trump 'cannot be trusted to honor the Constitution'
item.description: Television personality Geraldo Rivera revealed Monday that he is backing Vice President Kamala Harris over former President Donald Trump in the election, now less than a month away.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KRON4

item.title: Massive meat recall includes hundreds of products sold at Walmart, Target, more
item.description: Check your fridge and freezer: A massive meat recall linked to possible listeria contamination has impacted hundreds of ready-to-eat meals sold at major grocery store chains across the U.S., including Walmart, Trader Joe's and Target.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, ABC 4

item.title: 6 people accuse Diddy of sexual assault in new wave of lawsuits
item.description: Sean “Diddy” Combs was hit Monday with a new wave of lawsuits accusing him of raping women, sexually assaulting men and molesting a 16-year-old boy.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Massive meat recall includes hundreds of products sold at Walmart, Target, more
item.description: Check your fridge and freezer: A massive meat recall linked to possible listeria contamination has impacted hundreds of ready-to-eat meals sold at major grocery store chains across the U.S., including Walmart, Trader Joe's and Target.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, KRON4

item.title: 6 people accuse Diddy of sexual assault in new wave of lawsuits
item.description: Sean “Diddy” Combs was hit Monday with a new wave of lawsuits accusing him of raping women, sexually assaulting men and molesting a 16-year-old boy.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: FEMA temporarily pauses aid in parts of NC over reported threats
item.description: The Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) temporarily paused aid in parts of North Carolina this weekend after alleged threats targeted the agency’s personnel aiding in recovery efforts following Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WGN 9

item.title: Republican lawsuits target rules for overseas voters, but those ballots are already sent
item.description: Trump and his Republican allies contend these ballots could be part of an elaborate scheme to steal the election from him, a claim for which there is no evidence. Their challenge comes as the voters who receive the ballots are increasingly from groups that are presumed to be Democratic.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Jury selection underway in the trial of a man accused of killing 2 teen girls in 2017
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS (AP) — Eight women and six men have been selected so far to a jury in the trial of a man charged in the Indiana killings of two teenage girls during a winter hike in 2017.
item.dc:creator: RICK CALLAHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What to know about shaken baby syndrome as a Texas man could be first in US executed over it
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — A Texas man this week could become the first person executed in the U.S. for a murder conviction tied to the diagnosis of shaken baby syndrome. Robert Roberson, 57, is scheduled to receive a lethal injection on Thursday for the 2002 killing of his 2-year-old daughter, Nikki Curtis.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump edges out Harris with early voters in battleground states
item.description: Former President Trump has a slight edge over Vice President Harris with early voters in battleground states, new polling shows, a promising sign for the Republican in the highly competitive presidential race.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, WGN 9

item.title: Man armed with rifle accused of threatening FEMA workers in North Carolina
item.description: A man accused of threatening federal disaster workers in western North Carolina has been charged, according to the Rutherford County Sheriff's Office.
item.dc:creator: Michaela Ratliff, WGN 9

item.title: FDA shakes up weight loss market as Mounjaro shortage ends
item.description: Federal regulators shook up the booming weight loss drug marketplace when they announced Mounjaro and Zepbound to be no longer in shortage after nearly two years.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, Fox 5

item.title: Country singer pauses concert as wife gives birth on tour bus
item.description: Country singer Brantley Gilbert's wife, Amber, welcomed their third child on a tour bus in the middle of his concert on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KRON4

item.title: NJ Transit operator killed, passengers injured after train hits tree
item.description: An NJ Transit train operator is dead and nearly two dozen passengers are injured after a train struck a tree on the tracks Monday morning.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Yakubovsky, KRON4

item.title: Group associated with polygamous sect builds fences on US Forest Service lands
item.description: A group in Colorado has fenced a parcel of U.S. Forest Service Land in an attempt to claim the land under the Homestead Act of 1862.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, Fox 5

item.title: Armed man arrested near Trump rally denies 'assassination attempt' claims
item.description: “I’m an artist, I’m the last person that would cause any violence and harm to anybody,” the man has since said.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump suggests using National Guard, military against 'enemy from within'
item.description: Former President Donald Trump in a Sunday interview suggested using the National Guard or the military on Election Day to combat what he described as potential chaos from “the enemy from within” – a group that Trump said includes “radical left lunatics.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, ABC 4

item.title: Jerry West becomes first three-time inductee as Carter and Billups enter basketball's Hall of Fame
item.description: SPRINGFIELD, Mass. (AP) — The stage was filled with Laker greats when Jerry West made history Sunday night by becoming the first three-time inductee to the Naismith Memorial Basketball Hall of Fame.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MAHONEY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Massive hole swallows 2 homes in Florida
item.description: The wild scene raised concerns about whether the ground collapse was caused by a sinkhole or water damage from sewage issues.
item.dc:creator: Austin Kellerman, ABC 4
